next_steps: Perform endoscopic ultrasound-guided or direct nasopharyngeal biopsy of the suspicious mass
Perform serum ACE and lysozyme levels
Obtain chest imaging (CT chest) to evaluate for pulmonary involvement
Perform urinalysis to evaluate for glomerulonephritis
Initiate urgent histopathological assessment with appropriate immunohistochemistry and special stains

Perform targeted endoscopic biopsy of the nasopharyngeal mass with tissue sent for histopathology, immunohistochemistry (including IgG4, CD20, CD3, and EBER in situ hybridization), and microbiology cultures
Obtain EBV VCA/EBNA titers and serum IgG4 levels
Perform CT of the chest to assess for pulmonary involvement (nodules, masses, lymphadenopathy)
Perform urinalysis and microscopic examination to check for active sediment (hematuria, casts) indicative of glomerulonephritis
Evaluate for systemic inflammatory markers (repeat ESR/CRP and complete metabolic panel)
